Tiny models are useful because they make ideas visible.

A language model learns to predict the next token.
A token can be a character, a word, or a subword.
In this first experiment, we use characters because they are easy to inspect.

Research radar reads papers.
Research radar retrieves context.
Research radar explains evidence.
Research radar should say what it knows and what it does not know.

Build first.
Read to unblock.
Document evidence.
Keep the pipeline small.
Make the tests pass.
